title: S3 misconfiguration & data exposure
summary: Find and fix common S3 misconfigurations — public buckets, missing encryption, over-broad IAM — in a realistic mini-account, then verify your fixes.
learningObjectives: Identify public S3 buckets via both bucket-level ACLs and bucket policies.
Enable Block Public Access at the account and bucket level — and understand why both matter.
Replace bucket ACLs with a single auditable bucket policy.
Enforce encryption at rest (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS) on all object writes.
Recognise over-broad S3 IAM permissions and replace them with least-privilege.

successCriteria.description: No bucket in the account allows anonymous public read.
successCriteria.verify: aws s3api get-public-access-block + bucket policy review on every Lab-tagged bucket.

successCriteria.description: Every Lab bucket has a bucket policy denying unencrypted PutObject.
successCriteria.verify: Bucket policy contains s3:PutObject Deny with s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption condition.

successCriteria.description: Every Lab bucket denies non-TLS requests.
The lab IAM user no longer has s3:* on Resource '*'.
successCriteria.verify: Inline policy on /lab/auditor scoped to the lab buckets only.

hints: Start with `aws s3api get-bucket-policy-status --bucket <name>` on each bucket — it tells you immediately which are public.
Block Public Access at the account level (PutPublicAccessBlock on the account, not the bucket) catches future mistakes too.
Encryption-at-rest by default isn't the same as *requiring* it — add a bucket policy denying unencrypted PutObject to force the issue.
Don't delete the buckets — the lab grades you on fixing them in place.
_learnerPolicy: LEAST-PRIVILEGE: bare IAM Statement[] applied as an STS SESSION POLICY on the learner's console (effective = ShieldSyncLabUser(admin) ∩ this ∩ SCP). The engine adds the Version envelope + a fixed control-plane guardrail-deny. Scope to exactly what THIS lab needs — anything not listed is denied. Keep minified <~1700 (2048 hard limit incl. guardrail).
